source: CIS AWS COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.1.0
title: Ensure Public Access to EBS Snapshots is Disabled

input_row.source: CIS AWS COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.1.0
input_row.title: Ensure Public Access to EBS Snapshots is Disabled
input_row.description: To protect your data disable the public mode of EBS snapshots.
input_row.rationale: This protects your data so that it is not accessible to all AWS accounts preventing accidental access and leaks.
input_row.audit: Perform the following to determine if a snapshot is shared publicly: From the Console 1. Login to the EC2 console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/ec2/. 2. In the left pane click Snapshots. 3. Select the snapshot then click Actions, Modify Permissions. 4. Confirm that the snapshot is set to Private 5. Repeat for any additional Snapshots, Regions and AWS accounts. If the snapshot is set to public refer to the remediation below. From the CLI 1. For each snapshot, run aws ec2 describe-snapshot-attribute \ --snapshot-id <snapshot-ID> \ --attribute createVolumePermission 2. Validate Group is not set to all.
input_row.remediation: Perform the following to set a snapshot to private: From the Console 1. Login to the EC2 console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/ec2/. 2. In the left pane click Snapshots. 3. Select the snapshot then click 'Actions, Modify Permissions`. 4. Click the radio button for Private 5. Click Save 6. Repeat for any additional Snapshots, Regions and AWS accounts. From the CLI 1. For each snapshot, run aws ec2 modify-snapshot-attribute \ --snapshot-id <snapshot-ID> \ --attribute createVolumePermission \ --operation remove --group-name all References: 1. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/EBSSnapshots.html 2. https://awscli.amazonaws.com/v2/documentation/api/latest/reference/ec2/describ e-snapshot-attribute.html Additional Information: 1. Snapshots are constrained to the Region in which they were created. To share a snapshot with another Region, copy the snapshot to that Region. 2. AWS prevents you from sharing snapshots that were encrypted with your default CMK. Snapshots that you intend to share must instead be encrypted with a customer managed CMK. 3. The public option is not valid for encrypted snapshots or snapshots with an AWS Marketplace product code.
input_row.references: 1. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/EBSSnapshots.html 2. https://awscli.amazonaws.com/v2/documentation/api/latest/reference/ec2/describ e-snapshot-attribute.html Additional Information: 1. Snapshots are constrained to the Region in which they were created. To share a snapshot with another Region, copy the snapshot to that Region. 2. AWS prevents you from sharing snapshots that were encrypted with your default CMK. Snapshots that you intend to share must instead be encrypted with a customer managed CMK. 3. The public option is not valid for encrypted snapshots or snapshots with an AWS Marketplace product code.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**

The audit process described in the input control involves checking if the EBS snapshots have public access enabled. This can be efficiently and accurately audited using AWS APIs. The key points leading to the decision are:

- The audit field includes AWS CLI commands that can be used to verify the snapshot attributes, specifically using `aws ec2 describe-snapshot-attribute` to check if the `createVolumePermission` attribute's `Group` field is set to `all`.
- These CLI commands can be mapped directly to boto3 SDK methods, specifically `boto3.client('ec2').describe_snapshot_attribute()`, allowing for automated verification.
- The control relates to access control settings of AWS services, which are typically exposed through APIs.
- There is no requirement for manual inspection or judgment that couldn't be automated.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_ec2_ebs_snapshot_public_access_disabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

To automate this control, the following steps should be implemented:

1. **Enumerate Snapshots:**
   - Use the `boto3` library to list all EBS snapshots using `boto3.client('ec2').describe_snapshots()` API call. Ensure to list snapshots across all regions and accounts if applicable.

2. **Check Snapshot Permissions:**
   - For each snapshot, use the `boto3.client('ec2').describe_snapshot_attribute(SnapshotId=snapshot_id, Attribute='createVolumePermission')` method to retrieve the attributes related to the snapshot's `createVolumePermission`.
   - Verify that the `createVolumePermission` does not include a `Group` with the value `all`, which would indicate public access.

3. **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
   - Pass: If no snapshots have the `Group` set to `all` within its `createVolumePermission`.
   - Fail: If any snapshot is identified with `Group` set to `all`, indicating it is publicly accessible.

By leveraging these steps, compliance with this control can be systematically checked across environments, ensuring EBS snapshot access remains restricted to intended entities only.
